text: We report a mechanistic investigation into alkene hydrofunctionalization reactions mediated by the photoredox catalyst 9-mesityl-10-methylacridinium (Mes-Acr+). Through direct detection of alkene cation radical intermediates, we confirm that the phenylthiyl radical rapidly oxidizes the persistent acridinyl radical, a key step that bridges the photoredox and hydrogen atom transfer (HAT) catalytic manifolds. Our results further indicate that diphenyl disulfide ((PhS)2) and thiophenol (PhSH) operate via a common catalytic pathway, initiated by the photolytic cleavage of the disulfide bond. Density functional theory (DFT) analysis of the HAT transition states reveals that PhSH possesses a significantly lower activation barrier for hydrogen atom donation compared to 2-phenylmalononitrile (PMN), attributed largely to differences in structural reorganization. During the early stages of the reaction, we observe that Mes-Acr+ undergoes off-cycle adduct formation, likely triggered by the accumulation of PhS−. Finally, observed kinetic discrepancies between PhSH and (PhS)2 as HAT sources suggest that the proton transfer step may exert a substantial influence on the overall reaction rate.
